name: Phil Schneider
description: Phil Schneider (1947-1996) was an American self-proclaimed structural engineer and geologist who gained notoriety in the 1990s for his lectures and videos detailing his alleged involvement in secret government projects and encounters with extraterrestrial beings. He claimed to have worked on deep underground military bases (DUMBs) and to have witnessed a battle between humans and extraterrestrials at the Dulce Base in New Mexico in 1979, during which he purportedly sustained severe injuries, including the loss of fingers. Schneider asserted that the U.S. government was secretly controlled by extraterrestrials and that a global conspiracy was underway. He presented various diagrams and documents as evidence, though their authenticity and veracity have been widely disputed. Mainstream perspectives dismiss Schneider's claims as unfounded conspiracy theories and pseudoscientific speculation. Skeptics point to inconsistencies in his accounts, lack of corroborating evidence from other individuals who supposedly worked at the same facilities, and the absence of verifiable documentation to support his assertions. His death in 1996 was ruled a suicide, but conspiracy theorists often suggest he was murdered to silence him. His claims continue to circulate within UFO and conspiracy communities, often cited as evidence of government secrecy and alien involvement on Earth.
subtitle: Alleged government conspiracy whistleblower.

claims.claim: Worked on Deep Underground Military Bases (DUMBs)
claims.evidence: Schneider claimed to have extensive knowledge of DUMBs and presented diagrams he said were blueprints.
claims.criticism: No independent verification of his employment or access to classified information has been found. His diagrams are often considered vague and lacking specific details that would confirm their authenticity.

claims.claim: Witnessed a battle with extraterrestrials at Dulce Base
claims.evidence: Schneider described a firefight with Grey aliens, resulting in injuries and the death of several others.
claims.criticism: No other credible witnesses have come forward to corroborate this account. The Dulce Base itself is largely considered a myth within mainstream scientific and government circles. The injuries Schneider claimed to have sustained are not definitively linked to the described event.

claims.claim: The U.S. government is secretly controlled by extraterrestrials
claims.evidence: Schneider presented this as a general conclusion based on his alleged experiences and knowledge of classified projects.
claims.criticism: This claim lacks any credible evidence and falls into the realm of unsubstantiated conspiracy theories. It is inconsistent with publicly available information and lacks any support from mainstream political or scientific analysis.

keyFigures.name: Alex Christopher
keyFigures.role: Conspiracy theorist who supported Schneider's claims

timeline.event: Schneider begins public lecturing about his alleged experiences.
Phil Schneider dies; ruled a suicide.
